archimate:Representation.name: RDF
archimate:Representation.documentation:
Description: RDF (Resource Description Framework) is a standard that enables businesses to represent and connect information in a structured and machine-readable way. It uses triples, which are subject-predicate-object statements, to describe resources and their relationships. RDF facilitates data integration and sharing across domains and systems, providing a common language for businesses to exchange and utilize structured information effectively. It forms the basis for building semantic web applications and supports data interoperability, enabling organizations to leverage and connect their data assets for improved decision-making and collaboration.
Publisher: W3C
Source: https://www.w3.org/RDF/
LOST view: SV-Functional Architecture Principles
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/RDFRepresentation
EIRA concept: eira:SolutionBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/RDFRepresentation
Interoperability Layer: Semantic
Realises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DataRepresentation

properties.value: RDF (Resource Description Framework) is a standard that enables businesses to represent and connect information in a structured and machine-readable way. It uses triples, which are subject-predicate-object statements, to describe resources and their relationships. RDF facilitates data integration and sharing across domains and systems, providing a common language for businesses to exchange and utilize structured information effectively. It forms the basis for building semantic web applications and supports data interoperability, enabling organizations to leverage and connect their data assets for improved decision-making and collaboration.
